# Kerberos Integration Test Infrastructure

This directory contains the Docker-based Kerberos KDC used by the HMS catalog
integration tests in `crates/sail-catalog-hms/tests/`.

## Architecture

Three containers run on a shared Docker network:

| Container      | Hostname            | Role                                       |
|----------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `sail-kerberos-kdc` | `sail-kerberos-kdc` | MIT KDC + admin server, creates principals and keytabs |
| `sail-krb-hms-*`    | `sail-kerberos-hms` | Apache Hive Metastore 3.1.3 with Kerberos SASL enabled  |
| *(test runner)*      | *(CI host)*         | Rust test process, acts as the GSSAPI client             |

The test runner (on the CI host) connects to HMS over Thrift using Kerberos
authentication. The KDC and HMS containers communicate over the Docker network
using their container hostnames.

## Files

- **`Dockerfile`** — Builds a Debian Bookworm image with `krb5-kdc` and
  `krb5-admin-server`. The KDC database is created at startup.
- **`kdc-entrypoint.sh`** — Container entrypoint. Generates `/etc/krb5.conf`
  and `/etc/krb5kdc/kdc.conf`, creates the KDC database, then starts
  `krb5kdc` and `kadmind`.
- **`bootstrap-kdc.sh`** — Called at test setup time via `docker exec`. Creates
  service and client principals, generates keytabs, and writes an
  `krb5.conf` to `/artifacts/` for the HMS container to use.

## Kerberos Configuration Choices

### `localhost` as the service principal hostname

The test uses `hive-metastore/localhost@SAIL.TEST` as the service principal
instead of the Docker container hostname (`sail-kerberos-hms`). This avoids
the GSSAPI `GSS_C_NT_HOSTBASED_SERVICE` name type performing forward/reverse
DNS lookups on the container hostname, which would resolve to the CI runner's
internal FQDN (e.g. `*.internal.cloudapp.net` on Azure) and fail to match
the principal in the KDC database.

### `krb5.conf` settings explained

The `[libdefaults]` section in the client's `krb5.conf` (generated by
`host_krb5_conf()` in `tests/common/mod.rs`) includes several flags that are
essential for CI environments:

| Setting                        | Purpose |
|-------------------------------|---------|
| `dns_lookup_kdc = false`      | Don't use DNS SRV to find the KDC; use the explicit `[realms]` block |
| `dns_lookup_realm = false`    | Don't use DNS TXT to map hostnames to realms |
| `rdns = false`                | Don't perform reverse DNS lookups on hostnames |
| `dns_canonicalize_hostname = false` | Don't resolve hostnames via `getaddrinfo()`/`getnameinfo()` |
| `qualify_shortname = ""`      | Don't append the DNS search domain to single-component hostnames |
| `ignore_acceptor_hostname = true` | Server accepts any hostname in the keytab matching service+realm |

See [Principal names and DNS](https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-latest/doc/admin/princ_dns.html)
and [krb5.conf](https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-latest/doc/admin/conf_files/krb5_conf.html)
in the MIT Kerberos documentation for details.

**Why `qualify_shortname = ""` is needed:** When `dns_canonicalize_hostname`
is `false`, MIT Kerberos still appends the system's primary DNS search domain
to any "short" hostname (one without a dot). On Azure CI runners, the search
domain is something like `<guid>.<region>.internal.cloudapp.net`, so
`localhost` would become `localhost.<guid>.<region>.internal.cloudapp.net`.
Setting `qualify_shortname = ""` (empty string) disables this expansion.
This option was added in MIT Kerberos 1.18.

### `[domain_realm]` overrides

The client's `krb5.conf` includes `[domain_realm]` entries mapping Azure CI
domains (`.internal.cloudapp.net`, `.cloudapp.net`) and local domains
(`localhost`, `.local`) to `SAIL.TEST`. This ensures that even if any hostname
leaks through the canonicalization safeguards, the Kerberos library maps it to
our test realm instead of attempting a cross-realm TGT lookup.

### Server-side `krb5.conf` flags

The KDC and HMS containers' `krb5.conf` (generated in `kdc-entrypoint.sh` and
`bootstrap-kdc.sh`) include `dns_canonicalize_hostname = false` and
`ignore_acceptor_hostname = true`. These prevent the Java HMS server from
performing its own DNS lookups when validating incoming GSSAPI tokens.

### `KRB5CCNAME` with `FILE:` prefix

The test sets `KRB5CCNAME` with the `FILE:` prefix (e.g.
`FILE:/tmp/krb5cc-test_name`) because some Kerberos library implementations
require an explicit cache type prefix to correctly locate the credential cache.

### `_HOST` placeholder support

The production code in `src/provider.rs` supports the `_HOST` placeholder in
Kerberos service principals (e.g. `hive-metastore/_HOST@EXAMPLE.COM`),
replacing it with the hostname extracted from the HMS URI at connection time.
This is standard Hadoop-compatible behavior. However, the integration tests do
not use `_HOST` — they pass a fully-specified principal directly — to avoid
triggering DNS canonicalization of the extracted hostname in the CI environment.
